The city council last night au-
thorized the board of works to
grant a permit for the wooden
building for the Billy Sunday tab
ernacle and apple show for 80 days.
The building will be. 14S $50. ad-
Joining the armory on Second
avenue from Brown to Bernard.


Although it is not, at present
planned to maintain the great
wooden structure longer than aft
er December, it will jim all likeli-
hood be used for the national irri-
cation congress next summer. In
this congress several thousand
visitors will participate and if the
chamber of commerce undertakes
an entertainment on its usual large
scale it will need more room than
the armory affords. The Sunday
tabernacle will accommodate 5000
persons. Theh armory accommo-
dations are less than half that
number.


There is talk among business
men of securing a long lease on
the ground and wiving the build
ing a cement covering such as was
done with Davenport's and using
it for permanent auditorium.
